seoTitle: Granton vs Granville Harbour — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Granton and Granville Harbour. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Granton vs Granville Harbour: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Granton and Granville Harbour.
heading: Granton vs Granville Harbour — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Granton, Tasmania and Granville Harbour, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Granville Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Granton (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Granton or Granville Harbour safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Granton has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Granville Harbour scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Granton scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Granville Harbour scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Granton or Granville Harbour?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Granville Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
